metadata.title: Build a 3-Pass Prompt Refinement Demo with Claude

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Bonjour, Liam — in for Bear. How does build a 3-pass prompt refinement demo with claude? Can you explain it, Bear?

props.command: Build a 3-Pass Prompt Refinement Demo with Claude?

beats.label: Hook — random iteration
beats.narration: Most prompt iteration is random — try something, see what happens, try something else. The 3-pass refinement loop replaces that with a structured diagnostic: diagnose, target, preserve, verify.

shots.notes: Two paths. LEFT: 'Random Iteration' — arrows going in circles (prompt -> meh -> tweak -> different meh). RIGHT: '3-Pass Loop' — linear: Pass 1 / Diagnose / Pass 2 / Verify / Pass 3. Crimson on left, green on right. Teardown palette.

beats.label: Claude command
beats.narration: The diagnostic command pipes the prompt and its output into Claude and asks it to name the failure, target one change, preserve what worked, and write the revised prompt.

props.command: claude "Diagnose this prompt-output pair: what specifically failed? Target the single highest-impact change. State what to preserve from the original prompt. Then write the revised prompt." < prompt_and_output.md

beats.label: Python code
beats.narration: The script runs all three passes and produces a change log between Pass 1 and Pass 2, and between Pass 2 and Pass 3 — annotating what changed and why.

props.code:
import subprocess, sys
prompt = open(sys.argv[1]).read()
pass1 = subprocess.run(
  ["claude", prompt], capture_output=True, text=True
).stdout
diag_cmd = (
  "Diagnose this prompt-output pair: what failed? "
  "Target one change. Preserve what worked. Revise."
)
pass2_prompt = subprocess.run(
  ["claude", diag_cmd],
  input=prompt + "\nOUTPUT:\n" + pass1,
  capture_output=True, text=True
).stdout
print("PASS 1:\n", pass1)
print("DIAGNOSTIC + REVISED PROMPT:\n", pass2_prompt)

beats.label: 3-pass structure
beats.narration: Three passes, three outputs. Pass 1 shows the failure. The diagnostic names what failed. Pass 2 fixes exactly that. Pass 3 confirms the fix held.

shots.notes: Three vertical columns: PASS 1 | DIAGNOSTIC | PASS 2. Pass 1: text block with red annotation 'budget section too thin'. Diagnostic: 'add word count constraint for budget section'. Pass 2: text block with green annotation 'budget: 3 bullets, 80 words'. Linear flow arrows between columns.

beats.label: The change — different failure mode
beats.narration: Now run the same 3-pass loop on a different failure mode — wrong format instead of wrong scope. The diagnostic routes to a different fix, but the loop structure is identical.

props.command: claude "Diagnose this prompt-output pair: output is a prose paragraph but the task needed a table. Target the format fix only." < prompt_and_output_v2.md

beats.label: Changed output
beats.narration: Format failure: diagnostic targets the format only. Pass 2 produces a table. The content is unchanged. The loop made one targeted fix.

shots.notes: BEFORE: prose paragraph (3 sentences). AFTER: 3-column table with same content restructured. Arrow between them labeled 'format fix only — content preserved'. Green checkmark on table.

beats.label: Teardown angle
beats.narration: The 3-pass loop is not about prompting better — it's about diagnosing correctly. Most prompt failures aren't fixed by rewriting. They're fixed by identifying the one dimension that was off and adjusting exactly that.

shots.notes: Large text: 'Diagnose correctly. Fix exactly.' Below: 'Rewriting the whole prompt = drift. Targeting one dimension = control.' CRIMSON rule at bottom. Teardown palette.

beats.label: Next reel teaser
beats.narration: Next: a self-critique loop — ask Claude to critique its own output against specific criteria before you revise.

shots.notes: Clean: 'NEXT: Self-Critique Loop →' Minimal teardown style.

beats.narration_text: Here's what the body just demonstrated.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Build a 3-Pass Prompt Refinement Demo with Claude] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?

props.title: Build a 3-Pass Prompt Refinement Demo with Claude
props.subline: cancer biology, one mechanism at a time
